questions.question: The accounting equation is:
Which of the following is a current asset?
The double entry principle states that every transaction has:
options: One debit and one credit entry of equal amounts
One entry in the cash book only

questions.question: A trial balance is prepared to:
options: Show the profit for the year
Check the arithmetical accuracy of the ledger entries
Show the financial position of the business

questions.question: Which of the following is a capital expenditure?
Depreciation is defined as:
options: The increase in value of an asset over time
The systematic reduction in the value of a fixed asset over its useful life
The repair cost of an asset
The replacement cost of an asset

questions.question: The straight-line method of depreciation charges:
Which account has a credit balance?
A credit sale is recorded as:
The balance sheet shows:
options: Profit and loss for the year
Cash received and paid during the year
The financial position of a business at a given date

questions.question: Gross profit is calculated as:
options: Net sales minus cost of goods sold

questions.question: Which of the following is a revenue expenditure?
options: Purchase of computers for office use

questions.question: The cash book records:
A bank reconciliation statement is prepared to:
options: Correct errors in the cash book permanently
Explain differences between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance
Show the profit from banking activities

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a liability?
options: Money owed BY the business to others
An asset with long useful life

questions.question: The petty cash book is used to record:
Carriage inwards is:
options: An income item added to sales
The cost of transporting purchased goods to the business
The cost of delivering goods to customers

questions.question: The purchases day book records:
Which of the following is included in the trading account?
A debtor is:
options: A person who owes money to the business
A person to whom the business owes money
A bank that lends money to the business
A supplier of goods on credit

questions.question: Closing stock is valued at:
options: The lower of cost and net realisable value

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-current (fixed) asset?
The reducing balance method of depreciation:
options: Charges more depreciation in early years and less in later years
Charges less depreciation in early years
Does not affect the book value of the asset

questions.question: When a debtor's account is written off as a bad debt, the entry is:
The accruals concept states that:
options: Revenue is recorded only when cash is received
Revenue and expenses are recorded when earned or incurred, not when cash changes hands
Expenses are recorded only when paid
Profit equals cash received minus cash paid

questions.question: A provision for bad debts is created to:
options: Anticipate possible future bad debts and match them against current income

questions.question: Which of the following is shown in the profit and loss account?
The going concern concept assumes that:
options: The business will close down soon
The business will continue operating in the foreseeable future
All assets will be sold at market value
The business always makes a profit

questions.question: Carriage outwards is:
options: The cost of delivering goods to customers, charged to profit and loss account
Added to the cost of goods sold

questions.question: Which of the following correctly defines 'working capital'?
A return inwards (sales returns) is recorded in the:
Which of the following is the correct treatment of drawings by a sole trader?
options: Debited to drawings account, reducing capital
Credited to drawings account, increasing capital
Credited to the cash account only

questions.question: Prepaid expenses are shown in the balance sheet as:
Accrued expenses are shown in the balance sheet as:
Which of the following is a source document in accounting?
The imprest system in petty cash means:
options: The petty cashier keeps unlimited cash
The petty cash float is restored to a fixed amount at regular intervals
All payments are made by cheque
The petty cash is used only for salaries

questions.question: Net profit is calculated as:
options: Gross profit minus all operating expenses
Gross profit plus other income only

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the consistency concept?
options: All transactions must be recorded at historical cost
The same accounting methods should be used from one period to the next
Revenue should only be recognised when cash is received
All possible losses should be anticipated

questions.question: The folio column in a ledger account is used to:
options: Record the date of each transaction
Show the amount of each transaction
Cross-reference to the book of original entry
Record the name of the person involved

questions.question: A suspense account is opened when:
options: The trial balance does not balance
Stock is found to be missing

questions.question: Which of the following is a book of original entry?
In accounting, the prudence (conservatism) concept means:
options: Anticipate all possible losses but only recognise profits when certain
Record all transactions at current market values
Match income with expenses in the same period

questions.question: The current ratio is calculated as:
Stock turnover ratio measures:
options: How quickly a business pays its creditors
How many times stock is sold and replaced in a period

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of partnership accounts compared to sole trader accounts?
options: An appropriation account is used to divide profit between partners
A partnership never uses a trading account

questions.question: Goodwill in partnership accounts arises when:
options: A new partner is admitted or an existing partner retires
The partnership makes a large profit
The partnership takes out a loan
All partners agree to dissolve the partnership

questions.question: The acid test (quick) ratio is calculated as:
In club accounts, the income and expenditure account is the equivalent of the:
Which of the following is NOT an error revealed by a trial balance?
options: A posting made to the wrong side of an account
Complete omission of a transaction from both accounts

questions.question: The receipts and payments account of a club records:
options: Only income earned during the period
All cash and bank receipts and payments
Income and expenses on accruals basis

questions.question: In a manufacturing account, prime cost equals:
options: Direct materials + Direct labour + Factory overhead
Total factory cost including depreciation of factory

questions.question: Which of the following is a capital receipt?
options: Proceeds from sale of a fixed asset

questions.question: Return on capital employed (ROCE) is calculated as:
Which of the following is a disadvantage of the straight-line depreciation method?
options: Does not reflect the fact that assets are more useful in early years
Results in unequal charges each year
Cannot be applied to all assets

questions.question: In the absence of a partnership agreement, profits and losses are shared:
options: According to time devoted to the business

questions.question: The gross profit percentage (margin) is:
A revaluation account in partnership is opened when:
options: Partners decide to take out loans
Assets and liabilities are revalued when a partner joins or leaves
The partnership makes unusually high profits
The partners want to change the profit-sharing ratio only

questions.question: Which of the following is shown on the debit side of a partner's current account?
The manufacturing account shows:
options: The profit from selling manufactured goods
The cost of goods manufactured during the period
The financial position of a manufacturer
All expenses of a manufacturing business

questions.question: Which accounting concept requires that assets are recorded at their original purchase price?
Overheads in manufacturing include:
options: Indirect costs such as factory rent, electricity, and supervisors' wages

questions.question: Which of the following is used to calculate the debtors collection period?
